meta_title: For Founders: SaaS pricing to Solve can't finish projects (Beginner Idea List)
meta_description: Learn how to SaaS pricing and overcome can't finish projects. A Beginner-level Idea List using Antigravity and Claude Code.

content.seo_title: For Founders: SaaS Pricing to Solve Can't Finish Projects
content.seo_description: Stop stalling on your MVP. Learn to leverage SaaS pricing strategies with Antigravity and Claude Code to finally ship and monetize your software product.
content.answer_block: The inability to finish projects often stems from a lack of market validation. By implementing a concrete SaaS pricing model early, you force yourself to build features that provide actual value. This shift from 'building everything' to 'shipping for revenue' creates the necessary pressure to reach your MVP finish line.
content.intro:
The graveyard of abandoned side projects is paved with good intentions and endless feature creep. You started with a vision, but somewhere between the third refactor and the fifth 'must-have' integration, the momentum died. You aren't failing because you lack talent; you are failing because you lack the forcing function of a paying customer.

Real founders don't finish projects; they ship products. By anchoring your development cycle to a specific pricing tier, you transform your codebase from a hobbyist's sandbox into a commercial asset. It is time to stop building for yourself and start building for the market. Let’s turn your 'can't finish' cycle into a 'ship and scale' reality.

steps.title: Define Your Value Metric
steps.description: Identify the core metric your users will pay for. Avoid flat-fee pricing; choose a usage-based or feature-gated model that aligns with user growth.
steps.antigravity_prompt: Analyze my project idea and suggest three SaaS pricing tiers based on usage-based metrics. Ensure the tiers prioritize a fast MVP release.
steps.claude_code_command: claude ask 'Review my current features and identify the core value metric for a Tier 1 MVP pricing model.'

steps.title: Build the Pricing Gate
steps.description: Integrate a simple subscription logic that locks premium features. This creates a clear boundary between 'free trial' and 'revenue-generating' code.
steps.antigravity_prompt: Generate a clean Stripe integration boilerplate that handles basic subscription status checks for my app's core feature.
steps.claude_code_command: claude run 'Implement a middleware that checks for active subscription status before allowing access to the dashboard route.'

steps.title: Implement Lean Validation
steps.description: Add a 'Pricing' page that actually accepts emails or payments. Even if the backend isn't perfect, the presence of a checkout flow forces you to finish the user journey.
steps.antigravity_prompt: Create a high-converting pricing page UI component using Tailwind CSS that showcases three tiers: Hobby, Pro, and Enterprise.
steps.claude_code_command: claude run 'Create a pricing page route that links to a Stripe checkout session for each tier.'

steps.title: Automate the User Lifecycle
steps.description: Don't manually manage users. Use automated emails to nudge free users to paid tiers, keeping your focus on development, not operations.
steps.antigravity_prompt: Write a script to automate welcome and upgrade-nudge emails based on user activity thresholds.
steps.claude_code_command: claude run 'Create a cron job that checks for free-tier users who have reached 80% usage and triggers a promotional email.'

steps.title: Ship the 'Minimum' Viable Version
steps.description: Cut any feature that doesn't fit into the current pricing tier. If a feature isn't worth paying for, it doesn't belong in your MVP.
steps.antigravity_prompt: Review my current codebase and identify features that are unnecessary for the initial paid MVP launch.
steps.claude_code_command: claude ask 'List all non-essential features currently in the codebase that should be moved to a backlog to prioritize the launch.'

pro_tips: Always price your entry tier higher than you think; it filters out non-serious users who drain your support time.
Ship the payment infrastructure first; if you can't collect money, you don't have a SaaS, you have a prototype.
Use 'Feature Flagging' to hide incomplete features so you can deploy to production daily without breaking the user experience.
If a feature takes more than 48 hours to build, it's not an MVP feature—it's a roadmap item for the second quarter.
Monitor 'Time-to-Checkout' as your primary KPI; if it takes more than 3 clicks to pay, you are losing revenue.
content.next_steps: Deploy your MVP to a live URL, connect your Stripe test keys, and share your landing page with 10 potential users today.
content.conversion_block: You have the tools and the strategy to stop stalling. Every day you delay is a day you aren't learning from real market feedback. Stop refactoring and start selling. Ready to build? Join our AI Builder community → Start your first project today.
